name: Wild Mushroom Risotto
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Wild mushrooms (e.g., Porcini, morels, shiitake, Portobello, or other flavorful varieties); OR use 1 lb fresh wild mushrooms and 1 1/2 oz dried porcini
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the mushrooms: Clean them carefully under running water to remove all grit, and trim as needed. Cut into thin strips or small chunks based on size and variety. For dried porcini, soak in boiling water until pliable (about 30 minutes), rinse well, chop coarsely, strain through a coffee filter, and add strained liquid to the broth.
2. Heat olive oil and butter in a non-stick pan. Stir garlic for about a minute. Add prepared fresh mushrooms and salt; sauté stirring frequently until they release moisture.
3. Add soaked chopped porcini and continue stirring over medium heat until all mushrooms are tender and lightly colored around edges.
4. In a separate large non-stick pan, heat remaining olive oil. Stir chopped onion over medium heat until soft and slightly golden. Simultaneously, bring broth to a simmer, cover, and keep warm.
5. Add rice to sautéed onions and stir together briefly. Add Marsala wine and cook away for a few seconds.
6. Add white wine and cook it off, then add sautéed mushrooms and about one cup of hot broth. Stir gently to ensure the broth simmers with the rice.
7. When most broth is absorbed, add another cup or so, continuing until all liquid is used and rice is al dente (tender but still slightly firm). This should take about 20-25 minutes.
8. Finish by stirring in remaining broth, Parmesan cheese, and chopped parsley. Serve immediately in shallow bowls; pass additional Parmesan for serving.
